title: Tiny Time Travelers Thaw and Thrive
story: Imagine being frozen for thousands of years, then waking up like nothing happened! In 2021, Russian scientists made an incredible discovery. They found tiny creatures called bdelloid rotifers trapped in Siberian ice for about 24,000 years. story: A dazzling ceremony marked the crowning of Louis XIV as King of France in 1654. On June 7, the young monarch, known as the 'Sun King,' began his long reign. Louis loved grand displays and built the magnificent Palace of Versailles. He ruled for over 70 years, making him one of the longest-reigning monarchs in European history.
activity_question: What was Louis XIV's nickname?
bonus_fact: Louis XIV became king at just 5 years old, but didn't rule alone until he turned 23.

title: Kings Clash in Glittering Grandeur
story: Trumpets blared as two powerful kings met in a dazzling display of wealth and power. On June 7, 1520, England's Henry VIII and France's Francis I began their famous encounter at Balinghem, France. This lavish event became known as the Field of the Cloth of Gold, thanks to the shimmering golden tents and decorations that covered the meeting grounds.
activity_question: What was the famous meeting between the kings called?
activity_choices: The Field of the Cloth of Gold
activity_answer: The Field of the Cloth of Gold
follow_up_question: Why do you think the kings wanted to show off their wealth at this meeting?

title: Hawaii's Big Step Towards Freedom
story: King Kamehameha III of Hawaii made a bold move in 1839. On June 7, he signed the Hawaiian Declaration of Rights. This important document protected the rights of Hawaiian people and their land. It was a huge step towards creating a more fair and just society in the island kingdom.
activity_question: What did King Kamehameha III sign on June 7, 1839?
follow_up_question: Why do you think protecting people's rights is important?
